name: Machine Learning
description: Core concepts behind models that learn patterns from data.

group.definition: An individual measurable property of the data used as input to a model, such as a column in a dataset, often paired with a **Label** in supervised learning.
The target value a supervised model learns to predict, provided alongside the **Feature**s during training.
When a model learns the training data too closely, including its noise, and fails to generalize to new data — the opposite of **Underfitting**.
When a model is too simple to capture the underlying structure of the data, performing poorly on both training and test sets — the opposite of **Overfitting**.
An optimization algorithm that iteratively adjusts model parameters to minimize a loss function, using a step size set by a **Hyperparameter** called the learning rate.
Techniques that penalize model complexity to reduce **Overfitting**, such as L1 and L2 penalties.
A configuration set before training, like the learning rate used in **Gradient Descent** or tree depth, that controls how a model learns.
A method of splitting data into multiple folds to evaluate a model's performance more reliably and detect **Overfitting**.
